T0282.title: Account Warming
T0282.definition: Account nurturing refers to the cultivation process where underground actors simulate normal user behavior to increase an account’s activity level and trustworthiness.
T0282.description: Underground practitioners use automated scripts or manual methods to make accounts perform routine actions such as browsing, liking, commenting, and posting, so that platform algorithms classify them as legitimate active users. The nurturing period can range from a few days to several months, during which the frequency of operations is gradually increased to avoid triggering risk controls. Once nurtured, these accounts are used for subsequent illicit activities such as traffic diversion, engagement manipulation, and fraud. If an account is banned, operators immediately activate backup accounts to continue operations.
T0282.usageExample: Using AI to auto-reply to notes and comments, auto-engage to nurture accounts and intercept traffic, boosting note popularity.

references.title: 2024 H1 Internet Underground Economy Research Report
